title: penstemon eriantherus var. whitedii
intro: Penstemon eriantherus var. whitedii, also known as the Whitestem Beardtongue, is a variety of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections and inflammation. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Penstemon eriantherus var. whitedii can be made by infusing the roots and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
